summary: This listener is a first-time Democratic voter in Rockville preparing for the June 23, 2026 primary who has now asked, multiple days running, for substantive candidate-linked content: career-long voting records (Kramer specifically, all primary candidates generally), a concrete answer on Andrew Friedson as the fiscal-restraint candidate, an explainer on the new MCPS school rating system (added 2026-04-30), and a clear decision path on the Wootton High School closure question. Their open policy priors stay constant: skepticism toward high property taxes, opposition to increased education spending, support for non-essential spending cuts and fiscal discipline, and a clear interest in which Democratic primary candidates oppose sanctuary policy on illegal immigration.
themes: MCPS rating system explained | Listener 2026-04-30 directly asked for a deep dive on the new MoCo school rating system; show has gestured but never produced a dedicated explainer.
Andrew Friedson as the fiscal-restraint candidate | Listener 2026-04-29 asked plainly if Friedson is the discipline candidate; needs a single concrete yes/no answer with vote citations.
Wootton High School closure decision path | Asked 2026-04-29; show has acknowledged the question but never traced who decides, when, and where Wootton candidates land on it.
Career-long candidate records, not just recent session | Listener 2026-04-25, 04-26, 04-27 asked for old news / career history on Kramer specifically and on all primary candidates.

Side-by-side documented policy comparison across primary candidates | Listener 2026-04-24 asked which Democratic primary candidates oppose sanctuary policy and what the policy differences are across the whole ballot.
open_questions: Is Andrew Friedson the candidate who stands for fiscal restriction and spending discipline?
Why does MCPS want to close and move Wootton High School, and what happens next?
What is Ben Kramer's full historical voting record, including old news, not just recent sessions?
Who among the Democratic primary candidates actually opposes sanctuary policy on illegal immigration?
What is the new Montgomery County Public School rating system and how does it grade my child's school?
underserved_topics: MCPS school rating system mechanics — how the new score is calculated and what scores Rockville schools received
Andrew Friedson's specific fiscal record by line-item, not just final budget yes/no
Wootton High School closure decision timeline — who votes, when, and how those board members got there
Career-long Kramer voting record (2007-present) on consumer protection, judiciary, immigration, and education funding
Side-by-side candidate matrix on listener's actual ballot for sanctuary, fiscal, MCPS spending, and Wootton
avoid_list: "A listener asked us last week which Democratic primary candidates actually oppose sanctuary policy" as a setup line — used verbatim in 2026-04-28 ep1 and 2026-04-29 ep3
Evan Glass background-screening DHS bill as the sanctuary-policy example — used in 2026-04-28 ep1 and 2026-04-29 ep3
Montgomery County Council ICE-agent mask-ban ordinance as the sanctuary benchmark — used in 2026-04-29 ep2 (may be referenced descriptively but NOT as the framing hook)
"Two stories, properly worked through" / "not spreading thin across six" as an episode-framing device — used in 2026-04-29 ep1
Governor Moore's "Leave No One Behind" slogan contrasted with disability service cuts — used in 2026-04-27 ep1 and ep1.rewrite
ACA marketplace quiet-downgrade story ("thousands of Marylanders have been quietly downgrading") — used in 2026-04-28 ep1 and revisited 2026-04-29 ep3
Maryland's 90-day part-time legislature explainer ("they meet in Annapolis for ninety days, the rest of the time they're lawyers, teachers, small business owners") — used in 2026-04-26 ep3
The wave of 25 Maryland lawmakers retiring or running for other offices framing — used in 2026-04-26 ep1 and ep4
Beltsville Agricultural Research Center closure as a District 19 candidate-record hook — used in 2026-04-27 ep4
Listener's own frustration about promised deep-dives not delivering, narrated back to the listener — has shipped across at least five episodes; do not use as a framing device again
